notes: MITRE operates D3FEND, a knowledge graph of cybersecurity countermeasure techniques. D3FEND is the defensive counterpart to ATT&CK, cataloging what defenders implement rather than what attackers do.

match_nodes.description: D3FEND — A knowledge graph of cybersecurity countermeasures

data.description: MITRE D3FEND is a knowledge graph of cybersecurity countermeasure techniques. It catalogs defensive techniques organized by tactic (Harden, Detect, Isolate, Deceive, Evict). D3FEND maps defensive techniques to the ATT&CK offensive techniques they counter.
data.notes:
D3FEND is the defensive counterpart to ATT&CK. Technique IDs use the D3- prefix followed by 2-5 uppercase letters (e.g., D3-MH for Message Hardening, D3-SCA for System Call Analysis). Organized into 5 tactics: Harden, Detect, Isolate, Deceive, Evict. Maps directly to ATT&CK techniques via counters relationships.

D3FEND technique URLs use CamelCase ontology names (e.g., d3f:MessageHardening), not the D3- shorthand codes. The D3- code cannot be algorithmically converted to the URL — a lookup or search is required. Filed under control (not TTP) because defensive techniques are things defenders implement.

urls.note: D3FEND SPARQL API and ontology data

children.description: D3FEND technique identifier (D3- prefix followed by 2-5 uppercase letters)

data.note: D3FEND technique URLs use CamelCase ontology names (e.g., d3f:MessageHardening), not the D3- shorthand code. The D3- code cannot be mechanically converted to the URL path — use the D3FEND website search or API to resolve individual technique codes.
